title: Consecutive holes-in-one
description: KNYSNA NEWS - Winning the lottery is far...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Winning the lottery is far more likely to happen than hitting two consecutive holes-in-one, but Greg Alcock (78) achieved this incredible golfing feat a few weeks ago in a club competition at the Sedgefield Links Golf Course.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Winning the lottery is far more likely to happen than hitting two consecutive holes-in-one, but Greg Alcock (78) achieved this incredible golfing feat a few weeks ago in a club competition at the Sedgefield Links Golf Course."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Even though this golf course is graded as a Par 3 course, which is shorter than many other golf courses, this achievement still takes some beating - and loads of luck.","html":"Even though this golf course is graded as a Par 3 course, which is shorter than many other golf courses, this achievement still takes some beating - and loads of luck."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"On top of that, Alcock has, due to cancer, been blind in the left eye since his thirties, which he says affects his depth perception severely.","html":"On top of that, Alcock has, due to cancer, been blind in the left eye since his thirties, which he says affects his depth perception severely."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Alcock, whose handicap is 10 at Sedgefield Links and 22 at the Knysna Golf Course, told the Knysna-Plett Herald that he had never, in his wildest dreams, thought he would one day hit a hole-in-one, let alone two consecutively.","html":"Alcock, whose handicap is 10 at Sedgefield Links and 22 at the Knysna Golf Course, told the Knysna-Plett Herald that he had never, in his wildest dreams, thought he would one day hit a hole-in-one, let alone two consecutively."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In fact, he never, in his teens or early adult life, had an interest in golf, and took up the sport only in 2018.","html":"In fact, he never, in his teens or early adult life, had an interest in golf, and took up the sport only in 2018."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"My friends used to call me to play only when they were short in four-ball, to make up the numbers. I would then accept, to help them out and for fun, with a nondescript set of golf clubs, which I was gifted by one of them.","html":"\"My friends used to call me to play only when they were short in four-ball, to make up the numbers. I would then accept, to help them out and for fun, with a nondescript set of golf clubs, which I was gifted by one of them."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Later on, it turned out that I wasn't a complete mug with the clubs, given my little experience. Later, I visited Andrew at Scratch Golf in Knysna, who highly recommended that I change to Srixon irons (z355), which was the best thing I ever did.","html":"\"Later on, it turned out that I wasn't a complete mug with the clubs, given my little experience. Later, I visited Andrew at Scratch Golf in Knysna, who highly recommended that I change to Srixon irons (z355), which was the best thing I ever did."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"He also recommended, to enable consistency, that I stick with one type of ball - that being Srixon soft.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"What happened on the day?","html":"What happened on the day?"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Alcock says that he and his three friends, Doug Stevens, Lloyd Gosling and Gerhard Dreyer, were once again playing, in an indivual competion on a Wednesday, when he hit the ball off the 5th tee to the 5th green.","html":"Alcock says that he and his three friends, Doug Stevens, Lloyd Gosling and Gerhard Dreyer, were once again playing, in an indivual competion on a Wednesday, when he hit the ball off the 5th tee to the 5th green."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When the four of them arrived at the 5th hole, they all looked around for his ball - only to find it in the hole!","html":"When the four of them arrived at the 5th hole, they all looked around for his ball - only to find it in the hole!"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Naturally I was quite stunned and surprised to realise I had just hit a hole-in-one.","html":"\"Naturally I was quite stunned and surprised to realise I had just hit a hole-in-one."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"The same thing happened from the 6th tee - when we once again found my ball in the hole.","html":"\"The same thing happened from the 6th tee - when we once again found my ball in the hole."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When Greg Alcock (third from the left) hit two consecutive holes-in-one, his friends, from left, Doug Stevens, Lloyd Gosling and Gerhard Dreyer were present.","html":"When Greg Alcock (third from the left) hit two consecutive holes-in-one, his friends, from left, Doug Stevens, Lloyd Gosling and Gerhard Dreyer were present."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Up to that point I was playing reasonably well overall, putting and so forth, but from there onwards, I was just going through the motions, trying to process what had just happened - all while my mates kept congratulating me.","html":"\"Up to that point I was playing reasonably well overall, putting and so forth, but from there onwards, I was just going through the motions, trying to process what had just happened - all while my mates kept congratulating me."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Initially I wanted to keep it low profile, but they kept egging me on to tell the media about it, so here we are.","html":"\"Initially I wanted to keep it low profile, but they kept egging me on to tell the media about it, so here we are."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Back at the clubhouse there was great excitement from everybody because no-one had heard of such a feat before,\" Alcock said.","html":"\"Back at the clubhouse there was great excitement from everybody because no-one had heard of such a feat before,\" Alcock said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Both aces were achieved at Par 3 holes - 80 metres and 104 metres respectively - using a Srixon 3 soft ball, and using a Srixon z355 No 9 iron on the 5th and a Srixon z355 No 7 iron on the 6th.\"","html":"\"Both aces were achieved at Par 3 holes - 80 metres and 104 metres respectively - using a Srixon 3 soft ball, and using a Srixon z355 No 9 iron on the 5th and a Srixon z355 No 7 iron on the 6th.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Sedgefield Links Golf Course manager Joe Grobler confirmed Alcock was a paid-up member of the club who shot two consecutive holes-in-one on the 5th and 6th holes.","html":"Sedgefield Links Golf Course manager Joe Grobler confirmed Alcock was a paid-up member of the club who shot two consecutive holes-in-one on the 5th and 6th holes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Alcock received a payout from his insurance company for the achievement.","html":"Alcock received a payout from his insurance company for the achievement."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"What are the odds of this happening?","html":"What are the odds of this happening?"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Google indicates that only one professional golfer, John Hudson (then aged 25), playing in the 1971 Martini International Tournament - part of the British PGA circuit - has shot two consecutive holes-in-one; on the Par 3 12th hole (178m using a 4 iron) and on the Par 4 13th (284m).","html":"Google indicates that only one professional golfer, John Hudson (then aged 25), playing in the 1971 Martini International Tournament - part of the British PGA circuit - has shot two consecutive holes-in-one; on the Par 3 12th hole (178m using a 4 iron) and on the Par 4 13th (284m)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Google indicates that at tournament level, professionally, this hasn't happened since.","html":"Google indicates that at tournament level, professionally, this hasn't happened since."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Only three golfers in PGA history are said to have achieved two holes-in-one during the same round, but not consecutively. Apparently, only one Par 4 hole has been aced in PGA tour history - by Andrew Magee in 2001 at the Phoenix Open.","html":"Only three golfers in PGA history are said to have achieved two holes-in-one during the same round, but not consecutively. Apparently, only one Par 4 hole has been aced in PGA tour history - by Andrew Magee in 2001 at the Phoenix Open."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The odds of a professional golfer getting two holes-in-one in the same round are 1 in 76 million, and for amateurs 1 in 17 million in the same round."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It is estimated that the odds of a professional golfer achieving consecutive holes-in-one in a competition/tournament are well over a billion and a half to 1.","html":"It is estimated that the odds of a professional golfer achieving consecutive holes-in-one in a competition/tournament are well over a billion and a half to 1."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
